topics.title: Day 1: Career Path Overview
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 1: Career Path Overview is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Explore cybersecurity career paths
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Explain Day 1: Career Path Overview and its role in Career paths, roles, skills mapping
topics.interviewQuestion: What career paths exist in cybersecurity?
topics.interviewAnswer: Technical: SOC analyst, penetration tester, security engineer, forensic analyst, detection engineer. GRC: compliance analyst, risk analyst, auditor, privacy officer. Leadership: security manager, CISO, director, architect. Specialized: cloud security, ICS/OT security, application security, threat intelligence.

quiz.question: What is an entry-level SOC role?
What path focuses on policy and compliance?
What cert is good for GRC?
What role tests applications for vulnerabilities?
What path requires highest level cert?
Which best describes Day 1: Career Path Overview?
quiz.explanation: Day 1: Career Path Overview is a foundational cybersecurity concept that defines how systems work. It is a security principle, not a programming language or protocol.

quiz.question: What is the primary purpose of Day 1: Career Path Overview?
quiz.explanation: Security principles like those in Day 1: Career Path Overview typically span confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

quiz.question: Where does Day 1: Career Path Overview apply in real-world scenarios?
quiz.explanation: Day 1: Career Path Overview concepts are universal across all security domains.

quiz.question: Which certification exam covers Day 1: Career Path Overview?
quiz.explanation: Most major certifications touch on Day 1: Career Path Overview at some level.

quiz.question: Best way to gain hands-on experience with Day 1: Career Path Overview?
quiz.explanation: Practical application in a home lab is the most effective way to build skills. Reading alone is not enough.

quiz.question: Which skill is NOT directly related to Day 1: Career Path Overview?
quiz.explanation: While web development may use security concepts, Day 1: Career Path Overview is more directly tied to risk, response, and monitoring.

quiz.question: Scenario: You encounter Day 1: Career Path Overview during a security audit. What is your first step?
quiz.explanation: A professional approach to Day 1: Career Path Overview during an audit is to identify the issue and document findings for further analysis.

quiz.question: What is a common tool used with Day 1: Career Path Overview?
quiz.explanation: Depending on context, Day 1: Career Path Overview can involve firewalls, SIEMs, or packet analyzers.

quiz.question: Understanding Day 1: Career Path Overview helps prevent which type of attack?
quiz.explanation: The specific attack Day 1: Career Path Overview prevents depends on its context within the security domain.

quiz.question: How does Day 1: Career Path Overview relate to the CIA triad?
quiz.explanation: Most security concepts including Day 1: Career Path Overview can impact all three pillars of the CIA triad.

topics.title: Day 2: Skill Mapping
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 2: Skill Mapping is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Map skills to career goals
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Explain Day 2: Skill Mapping and its role in Career paths, roles, skills mapping
topics.interviewQuestion: How do you map skills to cybersecurity roles?
topics.interviewAnswer: SOC Analyst: networking, OS basics, log analysis, SIEM, incident response. Pentester: web/app security, exploit dev, Python, networks, OSCP knowledge. Engineer: cloud, automation, scripting, architecture. Compliance: risk assessment, policy writing, audit, frameworks (NIST, ISO). Soft skills: communication, writing, teamwork, curiosity.

quiz.question: What skill is critical for SOC analyst?
What skill is critical for pentester?
What skill is critical for cloud security engineer?
What soft skill is universally required?
What distinguishes senior roles?
Which best describes Day 2: Skill Mapping?

topics.title: Day 3: Resume Building
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 3: Resume Building is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Build security-focused resume
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Explain Day 3: Resume Building and its role in Career paths, roles, skills mapping
topics.interviewQuestion: How do you build a cybersecurity resume?
topics.interviewAnswer: Format: reverse chronological. Summary: 2-3 lines highlighting experience, certs, focus area. Technical skills: categorized (network, tools, cloud, programming). Experience: quantify results (reduced alerts 30%, managed 500+ endpoints). Projects: home lab, CTF, GitHub. Certifications: in progress and completed.

quiz.question: What resume format is preferred?
How should experience be described?
What section shows practical skills?
How should in-progress certs be listed?
What should the technical skills section include?

topics.title: Day 4: Job Search Strategy
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 4: Job Search Strategy is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Develop job search strategy
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Explain Day 4: Job Search Strategy and its role in Career paths, roles, skills mapping
topics.interviewQuestion: What is a cybersecurity job search strategy?
topics.interviewAnswer: Target roles matching current skills, not dream role. Tailor resume for each application. Use LinkedIn (set Open to Work, connect with recruiters). Apply to 5-10 jobs weekly. Follow up after 1 week. Prepare for interviews: technical (scenarios, tools) and behavioral (STAR method).

quiz.question: How many weekly applications is recommended?
What method answers behavioral questions?
What is a common first-round interview?
options: Phone screen with HR or recruiter

quiz.question: How long after applying should you follow up?
What LinkedIn feature helps recruiters find you?
Which best describes Day 4: Job Search Strategy?

topics.title: Day 5: Networking and Career Development
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 5: Networking and Career Development is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Network for career growth
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Explain Day 5: Networking and Career Development and its role in Career paths, roles, skills mapping
topics.interviewQuestion: How do you network effectively for cybersecurity careers?
topics.interviewAnswer: LinkedIn: optimize profile, post insights weekly, engage with security content. Twitter/X: follow researchers, participate in discussions. Discord: join security communities (InfoSec Community, The Hive). Local: attend BSides, OWASP, ISSA meetings. Conferences: volunteer to get free access, network intentionally.

quiz.question: How often should you post on LinkedIn?
What is the best conference for networking?
How do you get free conference access?
What is the best first step for career changers?
options: Connect with people in target role and ask about their path

quiz.question: What networking should you prioritize?
